channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: TET1 deficiency amplifies macrophage inflammatory signaling associated with Crohn's disease.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - To define the role of Ten-Eleven Translocation (TET) proteins in Crohn's disease (CD)-associated inflammation through integrative human and mechanistic studies. Publicly available CD transcriptomic...

item.title: Immunoinformatic-based design of a multi-epitope subunit vaccine against Ruminococcus torques using subtractive proteomics and molecular dynamics simulations.
item.description: Scientific reports - Ruminococcus torques is an anaerobic, Gram-positive gut bacterium that has been associated with gastrointestinal diseases including irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IB...

item.title: Intestinal Stem Cells from Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease Retain an Epigenetic Memory of Inflammation.
item.description: Cellular and molecular gastroenterology and hepatology - Intestinal epithelial damage and impaired repair are hallmarks of ulcerative colitis (UC), even after inflammation resolves. Intestinal stem cells (ISCs) can retain stable epigenetic changes after ...

item.title: Cordycepin Ameliorates Dextran Sulfate Sodium-Induced Ulcerative Colitis in Mice by Inhibiting IL-6/IL-6R-Mediated p38 MAPK and NF-κB Activation Through Adenosine A2A Receptor Stimulation.
item.description: Drug design, development and therapy - Current ulcerative colitis (UC) therapies often cause adverse effects, and novel treatments are urgently needed. Cordycepin (COR), a bioactive compound from Cordyceps militaris, shows anti-inflamma...

item.title: Nanospatially confined ROS eliminator produced in situ by engineered Escherichia coli Nissle 1917 for oral treatment of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of controlled release : official journal of the Controlled Release Society - Oral interventions using engineered bacteria are effective for preventing and treating digestive diseases, yet improving bioavailability remains challenging. This study constructed a probiotic phar...

item.title: Integrative Transcriptomics and Machine Learning Reveal the Association of CBX4 with Inflammation in Ulcerative Colitis as a Potential Epigenetic Regulator.
item.description: Biomedicines - Background/Objectives: Epigenetic factors are increasingly recognized to contribute to the pathogenesis of intestinal diseases, yet the precise mechanisms through which these factors influence ulce...

item.title: Renshen-Baidu-San restores epithelial-immune crosstalk and drives type 2 immune repair in ulcerative colitis: an integrated multi-omics study.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease of the colonic mucosa characterized by recurrent flares and difficulty achieving sustained remission. Renshen-Baidu-San (BDS), a classical ...
